Rationale: Superseded by products.identifier plus optional products.alternate_identifier; ISBN-10 and UPC/EAN equivalence are normalized during lookup.
Publication-oriented fields are consolidated on products for the current working scope.
Store access, role, active dates, and numeric authority limits belong to explicit membership.
The record controls taxable fraction, order, compounding, and effective dates.
The record is an independent auditable authorization event.
An exact-copy identifier is not another product or variant SKU.
Removed transaction_type and mutable void state; transactions may mix sales and returns and use open, suspended, completed, cancelled.
Added direction and pending/completed/removed states; returns and post-voids create new linked lines.
Authoritative store-and-variant quantities and moving average cost.
Append-only explanation of on-hand and availability movements.
Explicit reservation records tied initially to POS lines.
Administrative and shared-catalog boundary.
Separates physical till accountability from POS devices.
Preserves original and recount cash counts without overwriting.
Audits receipt originals, reprints, gift receipts, and correction receipts.
Reason no longer directly determines return-to-stock; actual disposition is stored on the return line.
Tax rates use decimal precision rather than basis points.
ADR-0003 establishes Merchandise Classes as the sole merchandising hierarchy.
Products use merchandise_class_id; Variants may override it.
Completed POS history snapshots the accepted merchandising classification.
Customer Requests and Staff Suggestions are first-class demand records.
Future Purchase-Order supply is committed to Product Requests separately from physical Reservations.
Demand and buyer-review state derive from Product Requests and Allocations.
Reservations may be owned by pos_line_item or product_request.
V1 uses an opaque nullable reference without a Customers table.
A Receipt may fulfil several Purchase Orders; linkage belongs at Receipt-Line level.
Labels now match the nine architecture domains.
